refinement_note: v2 narrows the check to only flag references to raw annotation directories (results/llm_annotations/, final_release/llm_annotations/) in baseline code. Derived features in data/llm_features/ and config.py LLM_FEATURES_FILE definitions are exempt because they represent the intentional opt-in integration point, not raw DeepSeek outputs. The previous v1 check falsely flagged config.py line 46 (LLM_FEATURES_FILE = DATA_DIR / 'llm_features' / 'llm_features_deepseek.csv') because the pattern 'deepseek' was too broad.
conclusion: LLM annotation artifacts are opt-in and not referenced by default baseline training scripts. No unguarded hardcoded paths to results/llm_annotations/ or final_release/llm_annotations/ found in baseline code. Config.py LLM_FEATURES_FILE points to data/llm_features/ (derived features), which is the documented opt-in integration point.
